title: Shiva Tandava Stotram
intro: Read Shiva Tandava Stotram with Hindi meaning. Chant this powerful hymn to receive the blessings of Lord Shiva.
description:
Shiva Tandava Stotram
Verse 1
Transliteration:

Jaṭāṭavīgalajjalapravāhapāvitasthale gale'valambya lambitāṃ bhujaṅgatuṅgamālikām |

Damaḍḍamaḍḍamaḍḍaman-ninādavaḍḍamarvayaṃ cakāra caṇḍatāṇḍavaṃ tanotu naḥ śivaḥ śivam ||

Meaning:

Whose neck is sanctified by the flow of water (Ganga) coming from the forest-like thick matted locks of hair, and who has a great snake hanging like a garland around his neck. Who plays the Damaru with the sound of "Damad-damad-damad-damad", He (Lord Shiva) performs the fierce Tandava dance, may He bless us with auspiciousness.

Verse 2
Transliteration:

Jaṭākaṭāhasambhramabhramannilimpanirjharī- vilolavīcivallarīvirājamānamūrdhani |

Dhagaddhagaddhagaj-jvalallalāṭapaṭṭapāvake kiśoracandraśekhare ratiḥ pratikṣaṇaṃ mama ||

Meaning:

My devotion increases every moment towards Lord Shiva, whose head is adorned by the waving curls of the water of the celestial Ganga river, which is swirling in the whirlpool of his matted hair; whose forehead plate is burning with the flame of the blazing fire (dhagadh-dhagadh-dhagad); and who is adorned by the young crescent moon.

Verse 3
Transliteration:

Dharādrahendranand
inīvilāsabandhubandhura- sphuraddigantasantatipramodamānamānase |

Kṛpākaṭākṣadhoraṇīniruddhadurdharāpadi kvaciddigambare manovinodametu vastuni ||

Meaning:

May my mind find pleasure in that Lord Shiva, who is the playful companion of the daughter of the mountain king (Parvati), whose mind is delighted by the entire series of the glittering universe; who stops unbearable calamities with a mere flow of his compassionate look; and who has the directions as his clothes.

Verse 4
Transliteration:

Jaṭābhujaṅgapiṅgalasphuratphaṇāmaṇiprabhā- kadambakuṅkumadravapraliptadigvadhūmukhe |

Madāndhasindhurasphuratvaguttarīyamedure manovinodadbhutaṃ bibhartu bhūtabhartari ||

Meaning:

May I find wonderful pleasure in Lord Shiva, the sustainer of all living beings, whose face of the damsel-like directions is smeared with the cluster of radiance from the gems on the hoods of the reddish-brown snakes in his matted hair; and who is covered with the skin of a maddened elephant.

Verse 5
Transliteration:

Sahasralocanaprabhṛtyaśeṣalekhaśekhara- prasūnadhūlidhoraṇīvidhūsarāṅghripīṭhabhūḥ |

Bhujaṅgarājamālayā nibaddhajāṭajūṭakaḥ śriyai cirāya jāyatāṃ cakorabandhuśekharaḥ ||

Meaning:

May the Lord (whose diadem is the moon, the friend of the Chakora bird) bestow upon us prosperity forever, whose footstool is made grey by the dust of the flowers falling from the crowns of all the gods like Indra, and whose matted hair is tied with the king of snakes.

Verse 6
Transliteration:

Lalāṭacaṭvarajvaladdhanañjayasphuliṅgabhā- nipītapañcasāyakaṃ namannilimpanāyakam |

Sudhāmayūkhलेखया virājamānaśekharaṃ mahākapālisampade śirojaṭālamastunaḥ ||

Meaning:

Let the matted locks of Shiva bestow upon us the wealth of the great skull; who consumed the God of Love with the sparks of the fire burning on his forehead; who is bowed down to by the leaders of the gods; and whose head is adorned with the nectar-rayed crescent moon.

Verse 7
Transliteration:

Karālabhālapaṭṭikādhagaddhagaddhagajjvala- ddhanañjayādharīkṛtapra-caṇḍapaañcasāyake |

Dharādrahendranandinīkucāgracitrapatraka- prakalpanaikashilpini trilocane ratirma mama ||

Meaning:

My devotion is towards the Three-Eyed Lord, who subdued the fierce God of Love with the fire burning (dhagadh-dhagadh-dhagad) on his terrible forehead plate; and who is the sole artist capable of drawing artistic pictures on the tips of the breasts of the daughter of the mountain king.

Verse 8
Transliteration:

Navīnameghamaṇḍalīniruddhadurdharasphura- tkuhūniśīthinītamaḥ prabandhabaddhakandharaḥ |

Nilimpanirjharī dharastanotu kṛttisindhuraḥ kalānidhānabandhuraḥ śriyaṃ jagandurandharaḥ ||

Meaning:

May the Lord (who is the bearer of the Ganga, the wearer of the elephant skin, the friend of the moon, and the burden-bearer of the universe) bestow prosperity upon us; whose neck is bound with the darkness of the night of the new moon, obstructed by the gathering of new clouds.

Verse 9
Transliteration:

Praphullanīlapaṅkajaprapañcakālimaprabhā- viḍambikaṇṭhakandharāruciprabaddhakandharam |

Smaracchidaṃ puracchidaṃ bhavacchidaṃ makhacchidaṃ gajacchidāndhacchidaṃ tamantakacchidaṃ bhaje ||

Meaning:

I worship Lord Shiva, whose neck is black like the luster of a fully blossomed blue lotus, and which is tied with the beauty of that neck; who is the destroyer of the God of Love, the destroyer of the Tripuras, the destroyer of the worldly bonds, the destroyer of the sacrifice, the destroyer of the elephant demon, the destroyer of Andhaka, and the destroyer of Yama (the God of Death).

Verse 10
Transliteration:

Akharvasarvamaṅgalākalākadambamañjarī- rasapravāhamādhurīvijṛmbhaṇā madhuvratam |

Smarāntakaṃ purāntakaṃ bhavāntakaṃ makhāntakaṃ gajāntakāndhakāntakaṃ tamantakāntakaṃ bhaje ||

Meaning:

I worship Lord Shiva, who is like a honeybee seeking nectar from the flow of the sweetness of the bouquet of arts of the all-auspicious Goddess; who is the ender of the God of Love, the ender of the Tripuras, the ender of worldly existence, the ender of the sacrifice, the ender of the elephant demon, the ender of Andhaka, and the ender of Yama.

Verse 11
Transliteration:

Jayattvadabhravibhramabhramadbhujaṅgamasphura- ddhagaddhagadvinirgamatkarālabhālahavyavāṭ |

Dhimiddhimiddhimidhvananmṛdaṅgatuṅgamaṅgala- dhvanikramapravartitaḥ pracaṇḍatāṇḍavaḥ śivaḥ ||

Meaning:

Victory to the Shiva, whose fierce Tandava dance is initiated by the order of the loud auspicious sound of the Mridanga drum playing "dhimid-dhimid-dhimid"; whose fierce forehead fire burns, emitting the "dhagad-dhagad" sound, through the movement of the snakes wandering in the sky.

Verse 12
Transliteration:

Dṛṣadvicitratalpayorbhujaṅgamauktikam-asrajo- rgariṣṭharatnaloṣṭhayoḥ suhṛdvipakṣapakṣayoḥ |

Tṛṇāravindacakṣuṣoḥ prajāmahīmahendrayoḥ samaṃ pravartayanmanaḥ kadā sadāśivaṃ bhaje ||

Meaning:

When will I be able to worship Lord Sadashiva, having a mind that remains equal towards a bed of stones or a bed of precious stones, towards snakes or garlands of pearls, towards precious gems or lumps of dirt, towards friends or enemies, towards grass or a lotus-like eye, and towards common people or great emperors?

Verse 13
Transliteration:

Kadā nilimpanirjharīnikuñjakōṭare vasan- vimuktadurmatiḥ sadā śiraḥsthamañjaliṃ vahan |

Vimuktalolalocano lalāmabhālalagnakaḥ śiveti mantramuccaran kadā sukhī bhavāmyaham ||

Meaning:

When will I be happy, living in a cave near the celestial river (Ganga), having abandoned all wicked thoughts, always carrying my hands in a salutation posture over my head, and chanting the mantra "Shiva" with eyes free from restlessness, and focused on the Lord who has the moon on his forehead?

Verse 14
Transliteration:

Imaṃ hi nityameva muktamuktamottamastavaṃ paṭhan-smaran-bruvannaro viśuddhameti santatam |

Hare gurau subhaktimāśu yāti nānyathāgatiṃ vimohanaṃ hi dehināṃ suśaṅkarasya cintanam ||

Meaning:

Whoever reads, remembers, or recites this excellent hymn every day, becomes eternally pure. He quickly attains supreme devotion to the Guru, Lord Hari (Shiva), and has no other path or destination. The contemplation of the auspicious Lord Shiva removes all delusions of the living beings.

faq.q1: Who composed the Shiva Tandava Stotram?
faq.a1: The Shiva Tandava Stotram was composed by the demon king Ravana. According to legend, he created this hymn to praise and please Lord Shiva.
faq.q2: What are the benefits of chanting the Shiva Tandava Stotram?
faq.a2: Chanting this stotram is believed to provide mental peace, boost self-confidence, and eliminate fear and negativity. It is also considered highly effective for attaining prosperity and spiritual growth.
faq.a3: Tandava refers to the divine and powerful cosmic dance performed by Lord Shiva, representing the cycle of creation, preservation, and destruction of the universe.
faq.a4: Yes, reciting it daily is considered extremely auspicious. Regular practice helps in maintaining a focused mind and receiving the blessings of Lord Shiva.
faq.q5: Is there a specific time to recite it?
faq.a5: While it can be recited at any time, it is traditionally believed that reciting it during Pradosha Kaal or on Mondays, either in a Shiva temple or at home, yields greater spiritual benefits.
